2603.10046.title: Gated Adaptation for Continual Learning in Human Activity Recognition
2603.10046.paper_summary: Wearable sensors in Internet of Things (IoT) ecosystems increasingly support applications such as remote health monitoring, elderly care, and smart home automation, all of which rely on robust human activity recognition (HAR). Continual learning systems must balance plasticity (learning new tasks) with stability (retaining prior knowledge), yet AI models often exhibit catastrophic forgetting, where learning new tasks degrades performance on earlier ones. This challenge is especially acute in domain-incremental HAR, where on-device models must adapt to new subjects with distinct movement patterns while maintaining accuracy on prior subjects without transmitting sensitive data to the cloud. We propose a parameter-efficient continual learning framework based on channel-wise gated modulation of frozen pretrained representations. Our key insight is that adaptation should operate through feature selection rather than feature generation: by restricting learned transformations to diagonal scaling of existing features, we preserve the geometry of pretrained representations while enabling subject-specific modulation. We provide a theoretical analysis showing that gating implements a bounded diagonal operator that limits representational drift compared to unconstrained linear transformations. Empirically, freezing the backbone substantially reduces forgetting, and lightweight gates restore lost adaptation capacity, achieving stability and plasticity simultaneously. On PAMAP2 with 8 sequential subjects, our approach reduces forgetting from 39.7% to 16.2% and improves final accuracy from 56.7% to 77.7%, while training less than 2% of parameters. Our method matches or exceeds standard continual learning baselines without replay buffers or task-specific regularization, confirming that structured diagonal operators are effective and efficient under distribution shift.
2603.10046.paper_summary_zh: 可穿戴傳感器在物聯網 (IoT) 生態系統中越來越多地支持遠程健康監測、老年護理和智能家居自動化等應用，這些應用都依賴於穩健的人類活動識別 (HAR)。持續學習系統必須在可塑性（學習新任務）和穩定性（保留先前知識）之間取得平衡，但人工智慧模型往往會出現災難性遺忘，即學習新任務會降低對早期任務的表現。這一挑戰在領域增量 HAR 中尤為突出，因為設備上的模型必須適應具有不同運動模式的新對象，同時保持對先前對象的準確性，而不將敏感數據傳輸到雲端。我們提出了一種基於通道級門控調制的參數高效持續學習框架，該框架利用凍結的預訓練表示。我們的關鍵見解是，適應應通過特徵選擇而非特徵生成來進行：通過將學習的變換限制為對現有特徵的對角縮放，我們保留了預訓練表示的幾何結構，同時實現了對象特定的調制。我們提供了理論分析，顯示門控實現了一個有界的對角運算符，與不受限制的線性變換相比，限制了表示漂移。在實證上，凍結主幹顯著減少了遺忘，而輕量級門控恢復了失去的適應能力，同時實現了穩定性和可塑性。在 PAMAP2 上，對於 8 個連續對象，我們的方法將遺忘率從 39.7% 降低到 16.2%，並將最終準確率從 56.7% 提高到 77.7%，同時訓練的參數不到 2%。我們的方法在沒有重播緩衝區或任務特定正則化的情況下，達到了或超過了標準的持續學習基準，證實了結構化對角運算符在分佈變化下是有效且高效的。
2603.10046.author: Reza Rahimi Azghan et.al.
2603.10046.authors: Reza Rahimi Azghan, Gautham Krishna Gudur, Mohit Malu, Edison Thomaz, Giulia Pedrielli, Pavan Turaga, Hassan Ghasemzadeh
